message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Fix blocking items: focus JS + button label">
Reminder: You have blocking items to fix before consensus:
1. Heydon: button label (needs item title in accessible name) + missing focus management JS
2. Carson: missing focus management JS

Please fix these, then re-run the pipeline and request re-review. You also still need reviews from Scott and Lea.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Now I understand the pattern. I need to:

1. Create a `someday-maybe-focus.js` script following the same pattern as `waiting-for-focus.js`
2. Include it in the `someday_maybe.html` template via a `{% block scripts %}` section

Let me also check the "Activate" button label issue — from the blocking feedback, Heydon wants the accessible name to be clearer. Let me look at how it's currently done and also check what Heydon typically expects.

Looking at the current template, I see the aria-labels already include the item title (`aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}"`), so the button labels seem correct. But let me check if the "Activate" label itself is the issue — perhaps it's not clear enough what "Activate" means to screen reader users.

Let me also check the waiting-for item labels for comparison — those use "Received" instead of a generic action name. Let me check what the Inbox page uses for similar "move to inbox" actions.

content.content: templates/someday_maybe_item.html:22:        <button type="submit" class="someday-maybe-item__activate-button" aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}">Activate</button>

content.text:
The team lead mentioned two blocking items:
1. **Heydon**: Button label needs item title in accessible name + missing focus management JS
2. **Carson**: Missing focus management JS

The aria-labels already include the title (`aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}"`), so that part seems fine. But Heydon may want the "Activate" button's visible text to be more descriptive, or perhaps the label format needs adjustment. Let me focus on what's clearly needed:

1. **Create `someday-maybe-focus.js`** following the waiting-for-focus.js pattern
2. **Add the script to the template**

Let me also clarify with the team what specific button label change is needed, but first let me fix the clear blocking issue (missing focus management JS).

git commit --amend -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add Someday/Maybe list (Step 10, US-GTD-6): domain, CRUD, activate, UI

Implement the Someday/Maybe feature for parking ideas that aren't ready for
action. Includes SomedayMaybeItem domain type (simple struct, no state machine),
database migration, repository with PgExecutor support, service layer with
transactional activate flow (creates InboxItem + deletes SomedayMaybeItem),
full route handlers with HTMX fragment responses, templates with BEM CSS,
focus management JS for WCAG 2.4.3 compliance, and 25 integration tests.

Also generalizes inbox_repository::insert_inbox_item to accept
impl PgExecutor<'_> for transaction support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Amend commit with focus management fixes

content.text: The commit is amended (now 2617a6d) with the focus management JS and improved button label. Now let me broadcast the update to the reviewers.

input.content:
UPDATED: Step 10 commit amended to 2617a6d with two blocking fixes:

1. **Focus management JS** (`static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js`): Added focus management script following the established pattern from `waiting-for-focus.js`. Handles:
   - Focus moves to next/prev sibling item after activate or delete
   - Focus moves to "Park an idea" input when list becomes empty
   - Focus moves to edit input when entering inline edit mode
   - Focus returns to Edit link after save/cancel
   - Empty state shows/hides dynamically (WCAG 2.4.3 compliance)

2. **Improved "Activate" button accessible label**: Changed `aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}"` to `aria-label="Move to Inbox: {{ item.title }}"` for clarity. The visible text stays "Activate" but the screen reader label now describes the actual outcome.

Also added `{% block scripts %}` to `templates/someday_maybe.html` to load the focus script.

Pipeline re-verified: fmt clean, clippy clean, 252 tests pass, 11 Playwright pass.

Please review and respond with CONSENSUS: APPROVED or blocking feedback.
input.summary: Step 10 fixes: focus JS + button label, re-review needed

routing.content:
UPDATED: Step 10 commit amended to 2617a6d with two blocking fixes:

1. **Focus management JS** (`static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js`): Added focus management script following the established pattern from `waiting-for-focus.js`. Handles:
   - Focus moves to next/prev sibling item after activate or delete
   - Focus moves to "Park an idea" input when list becomes empty
   - Focus moves to edit input when entering inline edit mode
   - Focus returns to Edit link after save/cancel
   - Empty state shows/hides dynamically (WCAG 2.4.3 compliance)

2. **Improved "Activate" button accessible label**: Changed `aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}"` to `aria-label="Move to Inbox: {{ item.title }}"` for clarity. The visible text stays "Activate" but the screen reader label now describes the actual outcome.

Also added `{% block scripts %}` to `templates/someday_maybe.html` to load the focus script.

Pipeline re-verified: fmt clean, clippy clean, 252 tests pass, 11 Playwright pass.

Please review and respond with CONSENSUS: APPROVED or blocking feedback.

content.text:
Both blocking issues are now fixed and the commit has been amended. Waiting for reviewer responses on the updated commit 2617a6d.

Summary of fixes applied:
- `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js` (NEW) — focus management following the `waiting-for-focus.js` pattern
- `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/someday_maybe.html` lines 55-57 — added `{% block scripts %}` to load the focus script
- `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/someday_maybe_item.html` line 22 — changed aria-label from "Activate:" to "Move to Inbox:"
- `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/tests/api/someday_maybe.rs` line 580 — updated test assertion to match new label
